title: Server Input Validation
constants: VALID_ID: RegExp = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/ — alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores only
functions: isValidId(id: any): boolean — typeof id === 'string' && VALID_ID.test(id)
provider_constants: FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDER_CONFIG_DIRS: Record<string,string> = { cursor:'.cursor', claude-code:'.claude', gemini:'.gemini', codex:'.codex', agents:'.agents', kiro:'.kiro', opencode:'.opencode', pi:'.pi' } (frozen)
key_relationships: validation.js imports BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS, DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS, FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS from download-providers.js
validation.js re-exports ALLOWED_PROVIDERS (alias DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS), ALLOWED_FILE_PROVIDERS (alias FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS), ALLOWED_BUNDLE_PROVIDERS (alias BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS)
interaction_flow: Caller → isValidId(id) → checks string type + VALID_ID regex → boolean
Caller → sanitizeFilename(filename) → regex strip non-[a-zA-Z0-9._-] → string
important_notes: All provider arrays are Object.freeze(). validation.js is a thin facade over download-providers.js, adding ID regex validation, type allowlist, and filename sanitization. FILE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS has 8 entries (cursor, claude-code, gemini, codex, agents, kiro, opencode, pi). BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_PROVIDERS has 2 entries. ALLOWED_TYPES is hardcoded in validation.js only.
